rxn_class_summary: Electricity-driven asymmetric rhodium-catalyzed oxidative cross-coupling of 2-acyl imidazoles with silyl enol ethers to give non-racemic 1,4-dicarbonyl compounds.
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the chiral Rh-bound enolate (Rh2-1a, E_p^ox = +0.52 V vs Ag/AgCl) generates a Rh-coordinated alpha-carbon radical; a second anodic SET after silyl enol ether addition gives an oxocarbenium that is trapped by MeOH; cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: Rh-bound secondary ketyl-type radical after silyl enol ether addition (IV)
Rh-bound oxocarbenium intermediate (V) after second SET

electron_flow: The chiral Rh-Lewis acid binds the acyl imidazole and deprotonation gives a Rh-enolate that is anodically oxidized (1.5 V lower potential than free enol ether) to a Rh-bound alpha-radical; this radical adds to the silyl enol ether, the resulting beta-OTMS ketyl radical is oxidized again at the anode to an oxocarbenium, which is desilylated/trapped by MeOH to release product, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Electrolysis of 1a with TEMPO (without 2a) gave TEMPO-adduct 7 in 92% yield with 92% current efficiency; the 2a-derived TEMPO adduct 8 was not observed, supporting trapping of the Rh-bound alpha-radical [Rh2-1a]·+ rather than a silyl-enol-ether-derived radical.

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction of 1a with DPE in absence of 2a gave three-component product 9 (51%, 99% e.e.) via [Rh2-1a]·+ addition to DPE, further oxidation and MeOH trapping, supporting the proposed cationic intermediate V.

probe_reagents.name: Rh2-1a (isolated Rh-enolate complex)
probe_reagents.observation: Synthesized, crystallographically characterized (shielding of re face by bulky TMS); cyclic voltammetry shows reversible-type oxidation at +0.52 V vs Ag/AgCl, ~1.5 V lower than the silyl enol ether 2a (+1.72 V) and the free acyl imidazole 1a (>2 V), identifying it as the species actually oxidized.

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a + 2,6-lutidine, 2a, Rh2, and Rh2-1a
probe_reagents.observation: Only Rh2-1a shows a low-potential oxidation wave at +0.52 V; free 1a and 2a require much higher potentials, demonstrating that catalyst-bound enolate formation enables the mild anodic oxidation.

notes: Chiral Lewis acid catalysis lowers oxidation potential of the substrate by ~1.5 V via enolate complexation, simultaneously preventing homocoupling by sequestering the radical and enforcing enantioselectivity (re-face shielded by bulky TMS group on the benzothiazole ligand). Stereochemistry of C-C bond formation occurs on the Rh-bound radical III; the second oxidation (III to V via IV) is proposed without direct kinetic evidence.
